channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: THE FREAK CIRCUS QUIZ
item.description: I recently worked on a small fan-made guide site for an indie visual novel / horror game community, and I wanted to share a few practical notes from building it. The project is not an official...

item.title: How to Use Python's logging Module Like a Pro — From Beginner to Production Setup
item.description: How to Use Python's logging Module Like a Pro — From Beginner to Production Setup Python's built-in logging module is one of those tools every developer knows about, but few use well. When I...

item.title: I Built a Django-Inspired Web Framework in Rust — Here's What I Learned
item.description: If you've ever used Django, you know the feeling: one command, and you have an admin panel, an ORM, form validation, middleware, session handling — everything just works. Then you try Rust web...

item.title: بهترین استاد لینوکس ایران کیست ؟مقایسه بزرگ مدرسین لینوکس فارسی
item.description: مقایسه بزرگ مدرسین لینوکس فارسی؛ تحلیل کیفیت آموزش، تجربه عملی و ساختار دوره‌ها من محمد محمدی هستم ، الان می تونم بگم که بیشتر از 10 سال هست که دارم در حوزه آی تی و بصورت ویژه و تخصصی حوزه دواپس و...

item.title: Claude Code: The AI Agent Quietly Rewriting How We Build Software
item.description: If you're a developer who hasn't tried Claude Code yet, you may have missed one of the biggest shifts in software development. Until recently, AI coding tools meant smart autocomplete. You started...

item.title: Build a Low-Cost Home SOC: Enterprise Security on a Budget
item.description: The Democratization of the Security Operations Center Historically, Security Operations Centers (SOCs) were the exclusive domain of large enterprises, requiring massive capital expenditures,...

item.title: Kakunin introduces cryptographic compliance shield to secure autonomous AI agents
item.description: Autonomous AI agents are powering everything from customer support to high-frequency trading—but as they gain more control, the threats grow sharper. Too many agent security stacks depend on...

item.title: I Let AI Write My Backend Code for a Week — Here's What Actually Broke
item.description: I told myself it would be fine. I had been using AI coding assistants for suggestions and autocomplete for months — and it worked great. So when a new project came up with a tight deadline, I...

item.title: The Developer's Guide to AI Translation Without Going Broke
item.description: Look, the Developer's Guide to AI Translation Without Going Broke I still remember the first time I looked at my translation API bill. Three hundred and forty-seven dollars. For one week. Just for...

item.title: 65% of Enterprise AI Failures Trace Back to Context Drift. The Fix Is Not a Bigger Window.
item.description: Nearly 65% of enterprise AI failures in 2025 traced back to context drift or memory loss during multi-step reasoning. Not model capability issues. Not hallucinations from weak training data. The...

item.title: The impossible dream of the universal remote
item.description: You don't really ever have to explain why a universal remote is a good idea. You have a bunch of stuff that needs controlling; this thing controls them all. Many companies have set out to build a...

item.title: Solid-state batteries still aren’t ready, but gels are
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on e-bikes, power stations, and how to work anywhere, follow Thomas Ricker. The Stepback...

item.title: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future
item.description: Tech leaders debate whether the Anthropic episode is a wake-up call for India’s AI ambitions.

item.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing’s demand
item.description: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

item.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
item.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, the export control directive that led to Anthropic cutting off access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was triggered in part by cybersecurity research from Amazon and...

item.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
item.description: Once again, AI proves to be an unreliable source of information about AI.

item.title: X-Men ’97 has what Master of the Universe is missing
item.description: In 2026, Marvel and Mattel are both releasing projects designed to capitalize on people's love for iconic animated heroes from their childhoods. Masters of the Universe has put a live-action...

item.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns that led Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday.

item.title: Sealed Super Mario Bros. sells for a record $3 million
item.description: A copy of Super Mario Bros., still in the box and sealed with its original sticker, just sold at Heritage Auctions for $3 million. That absolutely crushes the previous record of $2 million, also...

item.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
item.description: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

item.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to lay off a significant chunk of its Xbox division and is reevaluating the plans for its next-generation Project Helix console. It's apparently also considering...

item.title: Bose’s latest QuietComfort Ultra are $70 off, marking a new low price
item.description: If you’re planning on traveling anytime soon, Bose’s second-generation QuietComfort Ultra headphones are a great companion for long flights and train rides. Not only do they offer excellent noise...

item.title: My first 24 hours with Siri AI on the Mac
item.description: I turned off Siri on the Mac years ago and never looked back. Similarly, I found Apple Intelligence so fruitless I never engage with it. But the new Siri AI coming to macOS 27 Golden Gate has at...

item.title: Never Post’s Mike Rugnetta on the creative process and the value of reliable power
item.description: Mike Rugnetta is a writer, podcast host, producer, audio engineer, educator, musician, sound designer, and father. In short, the man wears a lot of hats. He's the cocreator and host of the...

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The...

item.title: My yard is dying, so I made an app for that
item.description: When I returned to my computer five minutes after giving Gemini a lengthy prompt, I had two things: a functional app in a preview window, and a message about a bug. "~ Channel is unrecoverably...

item.title: A Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
item.description: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to...

item.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
item.description: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

item.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people,"...
